subject: Intra-agent communications — synchronous consultation, handoff, and collaborative planning between Claude Code sessions
title: Intra-Agent Communications — agent_messages + Herald Daemon + Consult-Before-Deliver
what: Defines the intra-agent communication layer: how two Claude Code sessions (in the same or different spokes) communicate synchronously. The layer consists of: (1) the agent_messages Supabase table as the shared communication bus; (2) the Herald daemon which polls agent_messages and spawns CC sessions headless to respond; (3) three communication patterns: consult-before-deliver (ask before landing a lug), handoff (transfer an in-progress chain to another session), and collaborative planning (joint spec or design). This is the mechanism that allows an AP session to consult a Sentinel session before finalizing an architecture decision, or to hand off a chain when its token budget is exhausted.
why: Currently sessions are isolated — a session can deliver a lug to another spoke but cannot ask a question and wait for an answer within the same interaction. This forces guess-and-deliver patterns that produce misaligned lugs. Intra-agent comms enables consult-before-deliver: before landing a lug that affects another spoke, the originating session sends a message and waits for the target spoke's session to respond. It also enables handoff: when session A exhausts its budget mid-chain, it can hand the chain to session B with full context, avoiding the orphan-chain pattern.
audience: AP — initiates consultations before cross-spoke lug delivery
Herald daemon — the runtime that makes intra-agent comms possible by spawning responder sessions
Ozi — uses handoff to redistribute chains when budget is exhausted mid-run
Any session that needs synchronous input from another spoke before proceeding
use_cases: Consult-before-deliver: AP wants to deliver impl-lug to framework spoke; sends agent_message to framework Ozi; Herald spawns framework session; session responds with 'proceed' or 'modify X first'; AP delivers (or revises) lug

Handoff: Ozi session A claims chain-herald-impl-v1, completes 3/7 children, hits 80k token limit; sends handoff message to Ozi session B with completed_children[] context; session B claims the same chain and resumes from child 4
Collaborative planning: two sessions co-author a spec by exchanging messages through agent_messages; each appends to a shared draft in the thread
Spawned-by-herald session: a session started by the Herald daemon auto-exits after responding (spawned_by=herald flag in session-guard.json triggers this behavior)
Cross-spoke consultation: spoke A asks spoke B 'do you have ToolGraph entry for anthropic-haiku?' and receives a structured response without a full lug round-trip
patterns: agent_messages is the bus: all intra-agent communication flows through this Supabase table — no direct file writes between sessions
consult-before-deliver: SHOULD send a message and await response before delivering any lug that will require significant work from the receiving spoke

Herald spawns responders: Herald daemon polls agent_messages for rows with status=pending and to_spoke matching its spoke; spawns a CC headless session to respond; marks row status=responded after session exits
Thread model: a thread_id groups a multi-turn conversation; initiating session creates the thread_id; all follow-up messages share it
Handoff message carries full chain context: completed_children[], deferred_children[], current file_scope, chain_id — receiving session uses this to resume without re-reading all history
Spawned-by-herald auto-exit: Herald sets spawned_by=herald in session-guard.json before spawning CC; startup check detects this flag and configures the session to exit after first response

columns.thread_id: uuid NOT NULL — groups multi-turn conversations; initiator creates via gen_random_uuid()
columns.from_spoke: text NOT NULL — wheel_id of sending spoke
columns.to_spoke: text NOT NULL — wheel_id of target spoke
columns.message_type: text NOT NULL — consult | handoff | collaborative | response | ack
columns.message: jsonb NOT NULL — structured message body (see message_body_schema below)
columns.status: text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' — pending | responded | acknowledged | expired
columns.response: jsonb — populated by the responder session
columns.ttl_hours: int DEFAULT 2 — message expires if not responded to within this window

consult.question: string — the question being asked
consult.context: object — relevant lug IDs, file paths, or structured context
consult.response_schema: object | null — JSON Schema the responder should conform to; null means free-text

handoff.context_summary: string — 1-3 sentences on what was done and what remains

collaborative.draft_path: string | null — path to the shared draft file

response.in_reply_to: uuid — message id being responded to
response.answer: object | string — structured per response_schema or free-text
response.verdict: proceed | modify | reject | null — for consult responses

herald_daemon.purpose: Polls agent_messages for pending messages addressed to this spoke; spawns a headless CC session to respond; marks rows responded
herald_daemon.exit_condition: Herald-spawned sessions exit immediately after writing a response row to agent_messages; they do not continue to a full wakeup

constraints: agent_messages is the only permitted intra-agent communication channel — no direct file writes between sessions for coordination
Consult-before-deliver is SHOULD not MUST: sessions MAY deliver lugs without consulting when the scope is clearly contained; consultation is required when the lug has cross-spoke behavioral impact
A Herald-spawned session MUST exit after responding; it must not enter full wakeup or claim chains
Thread TTL: a thread with no activity for ttl_hours is expired; Herald stops polling for it; initiating session must retry with a new thread_id
Handoff messages must include a complete context_summary — a receiving session must be able to resume from the handoff message alone without reading all prior track entries
Response schema conformance: if a consult message includes response_schema, the responder must validate its response against it before sending
tests: Consult roundtrip: session A sends consult message to spoke B; Herald detects it, spawns CC in spoke B; spoke B session responds; session A reads response; thread status=responded
Handoff roundtrip: session A sends handoff for chain-X with completed=[C1, C2]; session B receives handoff, claims chain-X, resumes from C3
Herald auto-exit: Herald-spawned session has spawned_by=herald in session-guard.json; after sending response, session exits without entering wakeup loop
TTL expiry: message with ttl_hours=0 is immediately expired; Herald skips it; no session spawned
Response schema validation: consult with response_schema={type:object, required:[verdict]}; responder sends {verdict:proceed} — passes; missing verdict — fails
Multi-turn thread: session A sends consult, B responds, A sends follow-up consult on same thread_id — Herald connects them as one conversation

open_questions: Herald daemon deployment: always-on process vs. cron-triggered? Always-on requires separate infra; cron has up-to-30s latency per message.
Local fallback when Supabase unavailable: file-based message passing via WAI-Spoke/runtime/messages-inbox.json? How does Herald detect incoming messages without Supabase polling?
Consult-before-deliver enforcement: should this be a lug schema constraint (blocked_by=consult-thread-id) or a behavioral guideline only?
Security: Herald spawns CC with full permissions in the target spoke; what stops a malicious message from triggering unintended work? Message author must be in a trusted wheel_id list.

perceive: Read this spec. Check if Supabase agent_messages table already exists (query information_schema.tables). Read spec-herald-daemon-v1 if present for full Herald implementation. Read WAI-Spoke/WAI-State.json for current session_guard structure to understand spawned_by flag integration.
execute: 1. Create Supabase agent_messages table per schema above. 2. Implement send_message() and poll_messages() functions (tools/agent_comms.py). 3. Add spawned_by=herald flag handling to session startup: detect herald session type, configure auto-exit. 4. Implement Herald daemon poll loop (initially as a cron job, not always-on). 5. Implement consult-before-deliver workflow: send consult, poll for response (max 2min timeout), read verdict. 6. Implement handoff workflow: send handoff message, release chain claim.
verify: 1. agent_messages table exists in Supabase with correct schema. 2. send_message() writes a row and returns message_id. 3. poll_messages() for to_spoke returns pending rows. 4. Herald-spawned session detects spawned_by=herald and exits after one response. 5. Consult roundtrip completes in under 5 minutes (Herald poll + CC spawn + respond + read).
